title: onboard_bee hybrid apply: codex plugin-first always writes repo-local codex hooks (GH #22 P0-1, D1/D4/D5)
action: In skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs: (1) add --runtime passthrough flag (claude|codex|both, default both) at arg parsing (~:3014); (2) HOIST the codex hook projection out of the repoHooks umbrella (advisor R4): keep :3103 repoHooks=false under --plugin-source AND keep the claude repo-hook block (:2458-2465 settings.json merge) strictly behind repoHooks, but add a separately-gated codex-only path (pluginSource && runtime in {codex,both}) that vendors .bee/bin/hooks/ + runs mergeCodexHooks(.codex/hooks.json) (reusing the :2470-2479 logic); it fires from the PASSED --runtime, never recorded state (upgrade path); (3) typed blocked path (advisor R3): wrap the codex-hook apply in a typed blocked result {status:'blocked', reason naming repo-copy or hybrid retry, forceable:false} mirroring skillSync.blocked (:2667-2688) — a write failure must NOT escape as an untyped thrown {error}; test by making .codex a regular file; (4) CRITICAL self-erasure fix (advisor R1): in skills/bee-hive/scripts/plugin_distribution.mjs, scope the cleanup so .codex/hooks.json bee entries are NOT stripped when the run covers codex hybrid (PROJECT_HOOK_FILES :9, cleanHookConfig :205-233, collectProjectCleanup) — claude settings.json stripping unchanged; thread the runtime knowledge into the helper (flag or param); (5) if the managed/recheck set gains the codex hooks file, gate that inclusion on runtime in {codex,both} && pluginSource so claude-only installs never report codex drift (advisor R5); (6) sticky repo-hooks record no longer silently swallowed. Tests in skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs: plugin-source+runtime codex writes .codex/hooks.json + vendored hooks; plugin-source+runtime claude writes NO claude repo-local hook entries and NO codex files; injected hook-write failure (.codex as file) -> typed blocked, never untyped throw; cleanup-scoping unit check (cleanHookConfig leaves codex entries under hybrid, still strips claude). Sync every projection copy (.claude, .agents, .claude-plugin, .codex-plugin trees) byte-identically; regenerate release manifest if needed.

truths: plugin-first + runtime codex ends with .codex/hooks.json and vendored .bee/bin/hooks present, written before the apply reports success
plugin-first + runtime claude leaves claude exclusivity byte-identical to today (no repo-local claude hook entries, no codex files)
an injected codex hook-write failure yields a typed blocked apply — skills are never reported applied without hooks
the sticky repo-hooks record is honored, not silently swallowed, under plugin-source
all projection copies stay byte-identical and the manifest check stays green

verify: node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs && node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Hybrid codex plugin-first core shipped: hoisted runtime-scoped hook projection, typed blocked apply, cleanup codexHybrid scoping (advisor R1 self-erasure closed incl. the one-line installer wires), managed-set gating, sticky-record notices; independent verify green on main 720448f
trace.verification_evidence:
# cph-1 — done report (hybrid codex plugin-first onboarding core)

Worker: cph1-worker (sonnet, isolated harness worktree). Worker commit
`82b7684`, merged into main as `720448f`. 2026-07-18.

## What shipped
- `onboard_bee.mjs`: `--runtime claude|codex|both` flag (default both); hoisted
  codex-hybrid write path (fires on `--plugin-source` + runtime covering
  codex, from the PASSED flag only — never recorded state): vendors
  `.bee/bin/hooks/` + merges `.codex/hooks.json`; typed
  `{status:'blocked', reason, forceable:false}` preflight for filesystem
  collisions (fail-closed — skills never reported applied without hooks);
  `codex_hooks` managed-set key gated on `runtime∈{codex,both} &&
  pluginSource` (claude-only installs report no codex drift);
  `repoHooksTransitionNotices` surfaces a superseded sticky repo-hooks record
  instead of silently dropping it.
- `plugin_distribution.mjs`: `codexHybrid` param (default false = byte-
  identical legacy) exempting `.codex/hooks.json` from the plugin-first
  cleanup — closes the advisor-R1 self-erasure loop. Never inferred from the
  `runtimes` param (different axis); always caller-supplied; CLI flag
  `--codex-hybrid`.
- `install.sh` + `install.ps1`: thread `--codex-hybrid` into the distribution
  helper cleanup call under plugin-first + codex coverage (mid-task scope
  extension, orchestrator-approved: the one missing wire that closed the
  self-erasure loop the existing E2E scenario actually exercises; Windows
  patched for parity to avoid shipping the same bug there).
- 4 mirrors byte-identical, rendered trees regenerated, manifest regenerated.

## Verification (fresh command output)
Independent orchestrator re-run on main after merge 720448f:

```
node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs        -> PASS, failures: 0
node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs -> 38 passed, 0 failed
node scripts/test_lib_mirror.mjs                          -> 4/4 PASS
node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check                 -> 354 file(s) match
node scripts/test_installers_e2e.mjs --installer bash     -> 16 passed, 0 failed
```

## Deviations (accepted)
- Mid-task E2E regression surfaced the unwired cleanup flag; resolved by the
  approved one-line (per installer) wire instead of relaxing the test.
- install.ps1 parity added unrequested (symmetric; prevents the same
  self-erasure on Windows).
- Worker unbound its stale session-lane binding to unblock its local write
  guard — worktree-infra quirk, filed as an observation, no state damage.
